name: Israel
headline: Israel FLOOR CONFIRMATION at 0.0 / Critical — May 17 produces the most severe single-night civilian-protection failure in the current cycle: at least 36 Palestinians killed in IDP tent strikes at Al Mawasi (Khan Younis humanitarian zone) overnight May 17, with 4 incidents where parents and their children were killed together. UN Human Rights Office documents 96 Palestinians killed in strikes during night/early morning of May 17-18 alone, characterizing the pattern as indicating 'little, if any, care is being taken to protect the lives of civilians in Gaza.' Pattern now establishes 'IDP-camp-strike-pattern' as recurrent (4 separate Al Mawasi strikes in 24h; humanitarian zone designation contradicted by sustained strikes). CONCURRENT: Amnesty International (May 2026) published investigation finding Israel's systematic destruction of high-rise buildings must be investigated as war crimes of wanton destruction and collective punishment — a prosecutable conduct category. CONCURRENT: OCHA HSU #290 documents only 50% offload rate for aid trucks from Egypt at Israeli-controlled crossings (first 11 days of May). Already at floor (0.0); no score change available downward. Documentation expansion is the methodology-appropriate response. Two new methodology categories surfaced: 'IDP-camp-strike-pattern-establishing' (4 separate strikes in 24h at designated humanitarian zone) and 'systematic-civilian-infrastructure-destruction-prosecutable' (Amnesty high-rise report establishing war-crime prosecution pathway).

AWR.rationale: Floor sustained. A1 detection: Al Mawasi was a unilaterally designated 'humanitarian zone' by the Israeli military itself — awareness of civilian concentration was definitionally present, then disregarded. 4 separate strikes in a single 24h window at a known-civilian-concentration site is the canonical anchor-1 detection-disregard profile.

EMP.rationale: Floor sustained. 36 Palestinians killed in IDP tents in a single night; 96 killed overnight May 17-18 per UN HR Office; 4 incidents with parents and their children killed together (single-family annihilation pattern). E2 perspective-taking: the families targeted were sheltering in tents in a zone Israel itself designated as humanitarian — the perspective-taking failure is structurally complete. E5 follow-through: no documented post-strike harm-mitigation, no acknowledgment of family-annihilation pattern.

ACT.rationale: Floor sustained. AC2 proportionality: tent strikes against IDP-camp displaced civilians have no plausible military-objective justification publicly disclosed. AC1 responsiveness: 50% aid-truck offload rate at Israeli-controlled crossings (OCHA HSU #290) is institutional-action failure compounding the kinetic harm. Aid-delivery trajectory now WORSENING (25% April → 50% offload May, but volume context differs).

EQU.rationale: Floor sustained. Differential harm to Palestinian civilian population in IDP camps — populations already displaced once being killed in their displacement shelter. EQ5 historical-harm-acknowledgment: no acknowledgment of the cumulative pattern (Al Mawasi previously struck multiple times in 2025 per UN documentation).

BND.rationale: Floor sustained. IHL violations on civilian protection compounded: (1) IDP tent strikes violate Geneva Conventions civilian-shelter protections; (2) the designated-humanitarian-zone status is a unilateral Israeli designation that has been routinely violated by Israeli strikes; (3) Amnesty International May 2026 report establishes high-rise systematic destruction as prosecutable war crime of wanton destruction and collective punishment. B5 boundary-of-acceptable-use: the systematic-destruction pattern crosses into prosecutable conduct category. Carry-over: ICC arrest warrants jurisdiction active; Hungary ICC enforcement pledge from May 14-15 cycle.

ACC.rationale: Floor sustained. AB1 harm-acknowledgment: no acknowledgment of the IDP-camp-strike pattern; no investigation announced; no correction-willingness. AB3 transparency: UN Human Rights Office documenting the pattern externally because internal accountability mechanisms are absent. Amnesty May 2026 report adds external prosecutorial-evidence accumulation. Cumulative ACC carry-over: Netanyahu 60% territorial admission (May 16) remains unaddressed; 2,400+ ceasefire violations unaddressed.

SYS.rationale: Floor sustained. S2 long-term-impact: 4 separate Al Mawasi strikes in 24h, with parents-and-children single-family annihilations, establishes a systems-level targeting pattern affecting displaced civilian populations. S5 systems-of-care: the humanitarian-zone designation paired with sustained strikes documents systems-level bad-faith use of humanitarian categories. Cumulative: 72,744+ deaths (May 14 figure), 850+ since ceasefire, 2,400+ ceasefire violations, aid delivery at 25% (April) / 50% offload (May early). The systematic high-rise destruction documented by Amnesty is systems-level infrastructure-destruction beyond individual-strike scope.

INT.rationale: Floor sustained. I4 values-alignment: the most acute integrity failure documented this cycle — Israel itself designated Al Mawasi as a humanitarian zone and then conducted 4 separate strikes on IDP tents in that zone in a single night, killing 36 (including 4 families annihilated together). The stated commitment (humanitarian-zone protection) and the documented conduct (4 strikes on tents in same zone, same night) are in canonical integrity contradiction. I1 consistency-under-pressure: the integrity failure expands across May 17-18 with 96 killed overnight per UN HR Office.

evidence.summary: UN Human Rights Office (statement May 19): During late night of May 17, Israeli military strikes on IDP tents in Al Mawasi killed at least 36 Palestinians, including 4 cases in which parents and their children were killed together. Al Mawasi is an overcrowded area in western Khan Yunis where the Israeli military had unilaterally designated a 'humanitarian zone.' In the last 48 hours, Israeli military intensified strikes especially in North Gaza, Khan Younis and adjacent Deir al Balah, killing at least 96 Palestinians during night and early morning of May 17-18 alone.

UN Human Rights Office: pattern of strikes on Internally Displaced People's tents and residential buildings as well as on crowded hospitals; pattern indicating 'little, if any, care is being taken to protect the lives of civilians in Gaza.'

Amnesty International (May 2026): published investigation finding Israel's systematic destruction of high-rise buildings must be investigated as war crimes of wanton destruction and collective punishment. Establishes prosecutorial pathway for systematic-infrastructure-destruction category.

OCHA Humanitarian Situation Update #290: only 1 in 2 aid trucks from Egypt able to offload at Israeli-controlled crossings in first 11 days of May. 50% offload rate documents continued institutional-action aid-delivery failure.
UNRWA Situation Report #216: humanitarian crisis in Gaza Strip and Occupied West Bank — continued displacement, aid-dependence, structural collapse documentation.

Carry-over from May 16 floor confirmation: al-Haddad strike (Hamas leader plus wife, daughter, 7+ civilians killed); Netanyahu 60% territorial admission against ~53% ceasefire boundary; 72,744+ cumulative deaths; 850+ killed since ceasefire effective date; 2,400+ ceasefire violations; ICC arrest warrants jurisdiction active.

keyEvidence: May 17 overnight: 36 Palestinians killed in IDP tent strikes at Al Mawasi (designated humanitarian zone); 4 incidents where parents and their children were killed together.
May 17-18 overnight: 96 Palestinians killed per UN Human Rights Office.
OHCHR pattern statement: 'little, if any, care is being taken to protect the lives of civilians in Gaza.'
Amnesty International May 2026: systematic high-rise destruction must be investigated as war crimes of wanton destruction and collective punishment.
OCHA HSU #290: 50% aid-truck offload rate at Israeli-controlled crossings (first 11 days of May).
Floor sustained at 0.0; no further downward movement available; documentation expansion the methodology-appropriate response.
rationale: Floor confirmation at 0.0. May 17 produces the most severe single-night civilian-protection failure documented in the current cycle: 36 Palestinians killed in 4 separate strikes on IDP tents in a unilaterally-designated humanitarian zone, with parents and children annihilated together in 4 separate family-units. The UN Human Rights Office characterization ('little, if any, care is being taken to protect the lives of civilians') is the methodology's canonical floor-conduct external-determination signal. The Amnesty International high-rise destruction investigation establishes a prosecutorial pathway for the systematic-infrastructure-destruction category — significant for future ICC engagement. Floor scoring at 0.0 means no downward movement is mathematically available; the assessment expands documentation and surfaces two new methodology categories. Math: 8/8 = 1.0 → ((1.0-1)/4)*100 = 0.0.
watches: IDP-camp-strike-pattern continuation: monitor whether Al Mawasi or other designated humanitarian zones receive further strikes in May 18-25 window
Amnesty high-rise destruction investigation: monitor for ICC/national jurisdiction uptake of prosecutorial pathway
Aid delivery offload rate trajectory: 50% (early May) — monitor whether further deterioration
Hungary ICC enforcement mechanism (carryforward) — first actual enforcement test
Netanyahu 60% territorial admission (May 16) — monitor for further territorial expansion announcement
Family-annihilation single-strike pattern: 4 parent+children incidents in single night is methodology-novel; monitor for replication
carryForward: Netanyahu 60% territorial admission ACC-dimension floor signal
Hungary ICC enforcement pledge accountability-environment shift
Aid-worker fatalities (8 killed during ceasefire) carry-over
Cumulative 72,744+ deaths, 850+ since ceasefire, 2,400+ ceasefire violations baseline
OCHA aid-delivery deficit (25% April → 50% offload early May) trajectory
newConductCategoryNote: PROPOSED NEW METHODOLOGY CATEGORIES: (1) 'IDP-camp-strike-pattern-establishing' — when an actor conducts multiple strikes in a short window (4 strikes / 24h at Al Mawasi) on a designated humanitarian zone they themselves designated, the pattern of strikes on protected displacement sites establishes a recurrent targeting category distinct from individual-incident scoring. The single-family-annihilation profile (parents and children killed together in 4 incidents in one night) is a sub-anchor. (2) 'systematic-civilian-infrastructure-destruction-prosecutable' — when an established human-rights organization (Amnesty International) publishes a formal investigation establishing systematic destruction of civilian infrastructure (high-rise buildings) as prosecutable war crimes of wanton destruction and collective punishment, the prosecutorial pathway becomes a discrete methodology marker. Both Israel May 2026 events are canonical instances. Interaction with prior 'targeted-killing-of-designated-militant-with-confirmed-civilian-collateral' (al-Haddad May 15-16): Israel cluster now contains three distinct floor-conduct sub-categories within one week — single-target precision strike with collateral, head-of-state ceasefire-boundary admission, and IDP-camp-strike pattern. The accumulation establishes a 'floor-conduct-expansion-without-floor-uplift' methodology pattern: floor-state actors continue to surface new conduct categories that document additional dimensions of floor without producing dimensional movement.
boundaryWatchResolution: Not a boundary case. Floor sustained at 0.0.

math_check.validator_check: Math clean. Floor confirmation; no score change.

disclaimer: This assessment is based on publicly available information and does not constitute a formal Compassion Benchmark Certified Assessment.
